steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage hypothesizes that helicopter surveying leads to overestimates of cougar population density. The blank must complete an example that supports this claim—meaning it should identify a study that used helicopter surveying and reported an anomalously high density. The data table is needed to find that study.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Zanón-Martinez et al. / Choate et al. study area sizes: compares the geographic area of two studies, not density values. Study area size does not illustrate an overestimate. ✗
  • Choice B — Choate et al. maximum density of 10.24 individuals per 100 km², higher than several other studies: explicitly states that Choate et al. reported a density higher than others—exactly the kind of anomalously high result that would exemplify an overestimate caused by helicopter surveying. ✓
  • Choice C — Johnson maximum density of 2.78, lower than several other studies: a low density would illustrate an underestimate, which contradicts the hypothesis. ✗
  • Choice D — Davidson et al. density of 5.50 vs. Zanón-Martinez et al. lower density: compares two studies without linking either to helicopter surveying or to an unusually high density. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
